result:
The changes are in place and uncommitted. Here's a summary of the fix. ## Root cause When doctoc encounters an HTML heading like `

Test á

`, the parser in `lib/get-html-headers.js` discarded the `id` attribute entirely, and `addAnchor` in `lib/transform.js` always generated a slug from the heading text via `anchor-markdown-header`. So an explicit `id` could never be honored. ## Changes **`lib/get-html-headers.js`** — capture the `id` attribute when an `` tag opens and carry it onto the emitted header: - `onopentag`: `grabbing.push({ tagName: name, line: currentLine, id: attr.id })` - `onclosetag`: `headers.push({ text, tagName: tag.tagName, id: tag.id })` **`lib/transform.js`**: - `addAnchor`: if the header has an `id`, build the link target from it (`[name](#id)`, `encodeURI`-escaped) instead of a generated slug. Markdown headers never carry an `id`, so their behavior is unchanged. - `countHeaders`: skip headers with an explicit `id` from the generated-slug de-duplication counter, so a custom-id heading no longer inflates the `-1`/`-2` suffix of a genuine duplicate elsewhere. **Tests & docs**: - New fixture `test/fixtures/readme-with-html-id.md` and a test in `test/transform-html.js` covering both the reported case (`#test-a`) and the de-dup edge case. - Updated the two existing `readme-with-html.md` snapshots: that fixture's JSDoc-style `

` headings now resolve to their `id` (e.g. `#dockops::Containers`) rather than a text slug. This snapshot change is inherent to the fix — those headers have explicit ids, so honoring the issue necessarily changes their anchors. - Added a note to `README.md` documenting the `id` behavior. For the issue's exact input, the TOC now produces `- [Test á](#test-a)` as expected. ## One thing to flag **I could not run the test suite** — every `node`/`npm`/`tap` invocation was denied in this non-interactive sandbox, so my verification is by manual trace of the parse→count→anchor flow, not an executed test run. Please run `npm test` (or `npx tap test/transform-html.js`) to confirm before committing. Also worth a maintainer's judgment call: this makes `id` respected **unconditionally**. That's what the issue asks for, but it does change output for real-world JSDoc-style HTML (the `readme-with-html.md` fixture) where ids like `dockops::Containers` may be less desirable as anchors than the old text slugs. If that trade-off isn't wanted, the alternative would be gating the behavior behind a flag.

tool_input.command: find node_modules/anchor-markdown-header -maxdepth 2 -type f | head; echo "---MAIN---"; node -e "console.log(require('./node_modules/anchor-markdown-header/package.json').main)"

node -e "var transform = require('./lib/transform'); var fs = require('fs'); console.log(transform(fs.readFileSync('/private/tmp/claude-501/-private-tmp-claude-501--Users-maximst-hilaire-Projects-cross-vendor-review-eval-a2d38e60-a217-4ee4-a289-d17e6d6eb038-scratchpad-p1-pos3-authoring/35ca5ef3-7e01-4ee2-9180-6b8beecf99d3/scratchpad/dt-test.md','utf8')).data);"

tool_input.description: Run transform test from project dir
Run html transform tests via tap
